content: Reproducible-research pipeline pitfalls: the 'reproducibility crisis' in data science pipelines stems primarily from inconsistent documentation of provenance (which raw file produced which output row), not from analysis-method errors — this is directly analogous to needing an image_id/source_path column threaded through every derived table. Best practice explicitly called out: never overwrite/mutate original raw data files in place; always write derived outputs to new files/locations, and if raw data itself must be modified, 'save as' with a date-stamped suffix so earlier states are recoverable. CSVs are appropriate for capturing intermediate/final tabular outputs but need per-row traceability metadata (source file, run timestamp, calibration factor used) to stay auditable as pipeline complexity grows.
